topics.title: Ancient collision may have flipped the Milky Way
topics.summary: Astronomers say a head-on collision with a dwarf galaxy about 10 billion years ago may have tipped the Milky Way’s disc by more than 90 degrees. Supercomputer simulations helped explain the unusually slow motion of stars in its halo.
topics.discussionPrompt: Imagine learning our whole galaxy got knocked sideways before the solar system existed. Does that make the universe feel more chaotic, more impressive, or oddly comforting?

topics.title: Electric flying taxi targets passenger service in 2029
topics.summary: Vertical Aerospace publicly demonstrated its electric aircraft shifting from winged flight to a vertical landing. The company is pursuing certification for four-passenger service in 2029, while the UK government has also shown interest in military uses.
topics.discussionPrompt: If a flying taxi cost about the same as a regular cab, would you try it right away, wait for years of safety data, or happily stay on the ground?

topics.title: Museum turns Victorian workhouse history immersive
topics.summary: A revamped museum in Ripon is inviting visitors to experience the routines and harsh choices of a Victorian workhouse. Its exhibits explore poverty, welfare, and the lives of people who entered the system.
topics.discussionPrompt: Do immersive museums help history stick better than objects behind glass, or can role-play make serious history feel too much like entertainment?

topics.title: AI chatbots stumble on election advice
topics.summary: Researchers testing major AI chatbots during Hungary’s election found inaccurate, inconsistent voting guidance, including recommendations for parties that were not running. The study warns people not to treat chatbots as reliable substitutes for official election information.
topics.discussionPrompt: What everyday question would you trust an AI to answer without checking, and what kind of question would always send you looking for an official source?
